creator: gpejko@hotmail.com
text:
Problem Report ports/28193

xchat unable to connect to IRC server

Confidential
no
Severity
non-critical
Priority
low
Responsible
freebsd-ports@FreeBSD.org
State
closed
Class
sw-bug
Submitter-Id
current-users
Arrival-Date
Sat Jun 16 01:40:00 PDT 2001
Closed-Date
Mon Jun 18 15:20:18 PDT 2001
Last-Modified
Mon Jun 18 15:20:18 PDT 2001
Originator
Goran Pejkovski <gpejko@hotmail.com>
Release
4.3 RELEASE
Environment 
FreeBSD  4.3-RELEASE FreeBSD 4.3-RELEASE #1: Fri Jun 15 03:33:19 EDT 2001     root@:/usr/src/sys/compile/DSLKERNEL  i386
Description 
When connecting to server xchat doesnt send enough parameters for
 USER variable to server. I tried versions 1.6.4 and 1.7.1 from ports 
and problem exist in both versions. Here is output:

--- Connecting to eu.undernet.org (62.250.14.6) port 6667..
--- Connected. Now logging in..
--- AUTH :*** Looking up your hostname
--- AUTH :*** Checking Ident
--- AUTH :*** No ident response
--- AUTH :*** Found your hostname
--- USER :Not enough parameters     <-- this is the problem
How-To-Repeat 
Just try to connect to any IRC server :)
Fix 
I have to type:
/raw USER aaa aaa aaa aaa
and then the server let me in.
I know that this is not original xchat problem because I used
xchat 1.6.4 under linux and there were no problems.
Audit-Trail 
From: Anders Nordby <anders@fix.no>
To: Goran Pejkovski <gpejko@hotmail.com>
Cc: freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org
Subject: Re: ports/28193: xchat unable to connect to IRC server
Date: Tue, 19 Jun 2001 00:02:41 +0200

 Hi,
 
 Enter a username in X-Chat => Server-List. This should fix your problem.
 Entering something in both username+realname+nickname(s) would be even
 better. Also consider running an ident daemon.
 
 Does that help?
 
 Cheers,
 
 -- 
 Anders.
State-Changed-From-To: open->>closed 
State-Changed-By: jim 
State-Changed-When: Mon Jun 18 15:19:06 PDT 2001 
State-Changed-Why:  
This is pilot error.  Both xchat ports work properly when configured 
correctly. 

http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=28193 
 
Anders,
I filled up all fields (username+realname+nickname(s)) in
X-Chat => Server-List  as I always did since X-Chat 1.4.0 but that
doesn't help, that's why I reported this problem. I have been using X-Chat 
in various versions of Linux and FreeBSD 4.2 and I never had this problem.
I thougt that 1.6.4 from  ports have some problems, so I upgraded it to 1.7.1
from ports but I had the same problems. X-Chat does not need identd to work 
properly. BTW, ksirc works properly.
Jim,
What do you think under "pilot error" and "when configured correctly"?
There's not much to configure on X-Chat and it is not my first time using it.
If I am the only person with this problem you can consider it as pilot error,
and close this case. Can you tell me what  I have to configure, so my X-Chat
can work properly.
Thanks,
Goran Pejkovski
